cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Acxiom & EMARKETER Report: Fragmented Identity Data Is Blocking Enterprise Growth
stories.plain: A joint Acxiom/EMARKETER study finds that fragmented customer identity data is the top barrier stopping brands from using AI effectively across the enterprise. It argues identity resolution must be treated as a company-wide asset, not a marketing tool — a direct signal for how CDPs and data governance get prioritized and funded.

stories.head: AI-Driven Customer Intent Modeling Is Replacing Demographic Targeting in MarTech
stories.plain: This analysis argues that predicting what a customer will do next is now more valuable than knowing who they are. It maps how real-time behavioral signals, machine learning, and AI intent engines are replacing broad demographic segments — reshaping how marketing platforms are built and bought.

stories.head: Syncro Builds 35 Self-Serve Outbound Campaigns and $400K Pipeline Using ZoomInfo
stories.plain: IT software firm Syncro shifted from inbound-only to 35 self-serve audience-targeted campaigns in just months, generating $400K in pipeline. The case matters because it shows how AI-assisted audience building in a GTM platform lets small teams run scaled outbound without a data science department.

stories.head: Snappy Roughly Doubles Qualified Opportunity Rate Using Buying-Signal Timing on ZoomInfo
stories.plain: Corporate gifting platform Snappy replaced guesswork prospecting with signal-timed outreach, roughly doubling the rate at which early conversations become qualified opportunities and landing a multi-year Fortune 500 deal. It illustrates how agentic decisioning on intent signals changes conversion math for sales teams.

stories.head: Expert Mode: Why the Linear Marketing Funnel Is a Misleading Model for AI-Era Decisions
stories.plain: A practitioner essay argues the classic awareness-to-conversion funnel produces dashboard metrics that obscure real buying behavior, and that AI-era tools demand non-linear engagement models. Relevant for anyone deciding how to structure measurement and governance around AI-driven customer journeys.

stories.head: Gartner: Customers 3x more likely to resolve issues via ChatGPT than company chatbots; AI marketing ROI lags spend
stories.plain: Gartner research finds customers are three times more likely to use third-party AI tools like ChatGPT than a brand's own chatbot — and the function spending most on AI is showing the least return. This directly challenges how most martech budgets are allocated and raises urgent questions about where AI investment actually delivers value.

stories.head: Demand-Genius research: AI cites brands in only 16% of B2B responses, zero citations in awareness stage
stories.plain: New research shows that AI systems skip brand citations almost entirely during early B2B buyer journeys, with a 0% citation rate at awareness and consideration stages. Marketers relying on AI mention-tracking are measuring the wrong thing — conversational context and authority signals matter far more than raw mentions for being recommended.

stories.head: Pega's Tara DeZao on agentic AI as end-to-end marketing orchestration, not just content generation
stories.plain: Pega's marketing AI lead explains the shift from using AI to generate assets to deploying it as an autonomous orchestration layer that manages workflows, understands intent, and gives strategic feedback. This reframes how buyers should evaluate and procure marketing AI — moving from tool-by-task to system-level capability.

stories.head: AI adoption hits 90% in CX but organisations split on governance, architecture, and proving ROI
stories.plain: A new survey finds CX AI adoption is nearly universal, yet organisations are diverging sharply on how to govern it, which architecture to use, and how to build customer trust. For buyers and builders, this signals that governance and architecture choices — not adoption itself — are now the competitive differentiator.

stories.head: The CMS is becoming the AI operating system for brands: what to look for now
stories.plain: Choosing a content management system now means choosing the platform through which AI agents discover, personalise, and govern your brand content. The article lays out practical criteria for evaluating a CMS as an AI-ready foundation — directly affecting how marketing infrastructure is bought and built going forward.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: Agentic AI Orchestrates the Funnel End-to-End
body: The conversation has moved well past chatbots: AI agents are now being wired into buying-signal detection, outbound campaign sequencing, and real-time contact-center coaching. Tools like ZoomInfo's buying-signals engine and Cresta's training simulator show that the agent layer is no longer a prototype — it is live production infrastructure.

The strategic shift is from AI as a content shortcut to AI as an autonomous workflow runner. Marketers who treat agents as mere prompt interfaces will miss the real leverage: letting agents decide when to act, not just what to say.

themes.head: AI Search & Discovery Rewrites Measurement
body: When AI models answer queries directly, the classic click-funnel metrics — impressions, CTR, sessions — lose their meaning. Brand visibility now depends on whether an LLM's conversational context surfaces you favorably, not whether a keyword ranks on page one. Trust signals and authoritative content are becoming the new SEO currency.

Marketers need new KPIs: share-of-model-response, citation frequency, and sentiment within AI-generated recommendations. The Agile Brand piece on conversational context and the MarTech trust-visibility article both confirm that optimising for AI discovery is a distinct discipline from traditional search optimisation.
ev: Agile Brand · Conversational context & brand recs

themes.head: Fragmented Identity Data Caps AI's Potential
body: The Acxiom & EMARKETER report lands a blunt verdict: fragmented customer identity is the single biggest limiter of AI-driven growth. Without a reliable spine of unified identity, intent models misfire, personalisation degrades, and CAC climbs — a pattern echoed in the MarTech piece on rising acquisition costs.

Identity infrastructure is now a prerequisite, not a back-office concern. The Agile Brand Futurist note reinforces this: context — who the customer is across touchpoints — determines what the AI stack costs and whether it actually works. Investing in AI tooling before fixing identity is building on sand.
ev: MarTech Series · Acxiom & EMARKETER identity report
Agile Brand · Context decides stack cost

themes.head: The CMS Becomes the AI Operating System
body: Content management platforms are absorbing capabilities once spread across a dozen point tools: personalisation, intent modeling, structured data for AI retrieval, and now agentic publishing. The CMS is becoming the connective tissue between brand content and the AI systems that surface it — a decisive architectural shift for any martech stack rebuild.

Paired with the explosion of AI website builders (Cursor, Lovable, Bolt.new, v0), the implication is that the production layer of digital experience is being radically compressed. Teams that master this layer can iterate on brand presence at a speed that was impossible twelve months ago.
ev: MarTech Series · AI website builders landscape

themes.head: AI Adoption Is High, But Human Skill Atrophy Looms
body: With AI adoption in CX now touching 90%, a quiet crisis is forming: if AI handles execution, junior marketers never develop the judgment that senior marketers learned by doing. The MarTech piece on training tomorrow's marketers frames this as an organisational risk that has no technical fix — it requires intentional human development programs.

The investment hype cycle is also misfiring: the Agile Brand Futurist note warns that AI money is chasing easy automation wins while end customers want deeper, more meaningful experiences. The gap between what AI vendors promise and what buyers actually value is widening, and teams that close it will need human insight, not just better models.
ev: Agile Brand · AI investments chase easy money

month.takeaway: Across every layer of the stack, the 30-day signal is the same: AI capability is no longer the bottleneck — identity data quality, human judgment, and trust architecture are what separate winners from expensive experiments.
